data.title: Global Payments to acquire GTCR and FIS’s stake in Worldpay for $22.7 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Payment technology and software solutions provider Global Payments Inc. announced a final agreement to sell its Issuer Solutions business to FIS for US$13.5 billion and acquire Worldpay from GTCR and FIS for a net price of US$22.7 billion, with a total value of US$24.25 billion. It is reported that the transaction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2026. (Businesswire)

data.title: Today, the US Bitcoin ETF has a net outflow of 2,050 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF has a net outflow of 10,484 ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Lookonchain monitoring, 10 US Bitcoin ETFs had a net outflow of 2,050 BTC today, of which Fidelity had an outflow of 1,353 BTC. Fidelity currently holds 194,273 BTC, worth $16.33 billion; 9 Ethereum ETFs had a net outflow of 10,484 ETH, of which Grayscale ETHE had an outflow of 6,657 ETH. Grayscale ETHE currently holds 1,174,546 ETH, worth $1.86 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: According to Odaily, Lookonchain reports that today, ten U.S. Bitcoin ETFs saw a net outflow of 2,050 BTC. Among these, Fidelity experienced an outflow of 1,353 BTC, leaving it with a total holding of 194,273 BTC, valued at $16.33 billion. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs recorded a net outflow of 10,484 ETH. Grayscale ETHE accounted for 6,657 ETH of this outflow, with its current holdings at 1,174,546 ETH, valued at $1.86 billion.

data.title: Analyst: $75,000 may be the current defense area for Bitcoin bulls
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The market generally believes that any bearish trend caused by the unchanged Fed interest rate has been reflected in the Bitcoin price. Onchain analyst James Check pointed out that the real bottom of Bitcoin is at its real market average, that is, the average cost basis of active investors - about $65,000. The bears will try to defend the resistance level of $86,000, which increases the possibility of prices falling below $80,000 and hitting new lows. 
James Check added: "$75,000 may be the area where Bitcoin bulls defend in the current market. If the defense fails, the next step will be to return to the liquidation consolidation range to see how far it can go in the end." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: BTC falls below 84,000 USDT
data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 84,000 USDT and is now at 83,985.8 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 0.44%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: Global Payments to Acquire Worldpay in $24.25 Billion Deal
data.description: According to PANews, Global Payments has announced a $24.25 billion acquisition of Worldpay, with 45% of the shares coming from FIS and 55% from private equity firm GTCR. As part of the transaction, FIS will acquire Global Payments' issuing solutions business, valued at $13.5 billion. GTCR will complete the sale with 59% in cash and 41% in stock, and will hold a 15% stake in Global Payments post-merger, continuing to engage in future strategic developments. The deal is expected to be finalized in the first half of 2026.

data.title: Blackstone Group President Grey: If the tariff issue is resolved, the market will rebound very quickly
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Grey, president of Blackstone Group, said that the capital market has strong potential momentum, and if the tariff issue is resolved, the market will rebound very quickly. (Jinshi)

data.title: Market Sentiment Remains Bearish as Funding Rates Indicate Downtrend
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges (CEX and DEX) continue to indicate a bearish market sentiment. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are shown in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of the underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the trading platform collecting these fees. It serves to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring that contract prices remain close to the underlying asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% is considered the baseline. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it suggests a bullish market sentiment. Conversely, when the funding rate falls below 0.005%, it indicates a bearish market sentiment.

data.title: Circle Launches Refund Protocol, a Non-custodial Dispute Resolution Solution for Stablecoin Payments
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Circle announced that its Circle Research released the Refund Protocol, a smart contract that supports non-custodial custody and on-chain dispute resolution for stablecoin payments. It is built for the composability and transparency of on-chain commerce, and supports lock-up periods, arbitrator-mediated refunds, and early withdrawals based on off-chain protocols. In addition, the refund protocol also supports on-chain dispute resolution and USDC payment refunds without the custody or trust of centralized institutions.
